metadata.methodology: views/subscriber ratio proxy — not actual CTR. Outlier threshold: 3x channel median views/sub ratio. RealLifeLore included for title pattern volume only (format mismatch: animated). Subscriber counts as of early 2026.

example_titles: Democracy vs Republic: What's The Difference?
Capitalism vs Socialism: What's the Difference?
Rome vs Carthage: Why Carthage Lost
Federalism vs Nationalism: How Germany Was Made
USA vs EU: How Big Are They Really?
versus.note: proxy CTR from views/subscriber ratio — directional only. Versus pattern shows high variance: ideological versus titles outperform territorial versus titles. [USER: VidIQ CTR check recommended for top 3 examples]

example_titles: The Middle East's cold war, explained
Why the Mongols Were so Effective at Conquering
The Real Reason Why Rome Fell
How the African Slave Trade Actually Worked
The Weird History of America's Drug Laws
declarative.note: proxy CTR from views/subscriber ratio — directional only. Largest sample count — most reliable benchmark. Declarative titles without years perform ~1.3x better than those with years. [USER: VidIQ CTR check recommended]

example_titles: How The Soviet Union Collapsed
Why Switzerland is the Center of Europe
How 6 Companies Control 90% Of What You Read
Why Did the Roman Empire Fall?
How Africa Became Black and White
how_why.note: proxy CTR from views/subscriber ratio — directional only. 'How' performs marginally better than 'Why' in this niche (mechanism emphasis over causation). [USER: VidIQ CTR check recommended]

example_titles: Who Owns Antarctica?
Why Are There 2 Koreas?
Did Rome Actually Fall?
Who Actually Discovered America?
Was Hitler a Socialist?
question.note: proxy CTR from views/subscriber ratio — directional only. Question pattern underperforms versus/declarative in edu-history niche. n=9 is MEDIUM confidence — treat as directional only.

example_titles: Palestine: A History
Africa: A Political History
The Dark Ages: History's Biggest Lie
Medieval Europe: Myths vs Reality
Ottoman Empire: Rise and Fall
colon.note: proxy CTR from views/subscriber ratio — directional only. Colon pattern consistently weakest in niche — consistent with own-channel data showing -28% CTR penalty. Sub-categories with 'History:' or 'The X:' are worst performers.

territorial.note: Territorial disputes show highest ceiling but also highest variance. Map-centric thumbnails correlate with higher proxy CTR. Active ICJ/UN cases add ~0.5x multiplier on views. [USER: VidIQ CTR check recommended for outlier territorial videos]

ideological.note: Ideological myth-busting shows highest median CTR proxy — consistent with own-channel conversion data. 'Debunking' and 'myth' framing outperform neutral framing by ~1.4x on proxy CTR.

political_fact_check.note: Political fact-check content shows narrower variance than territorial — more consistent performer. Document-first framing (titles that reference source documents) show ~1.2x proxy CTR vs generic political titles.

Kraut.format_notes: Talking head + animated maps, 12-120 min, long-form documentary essay

Shaun.format_notes: Document-first, talking head, no animation, 40-90 min essay
Shaun.confidence_note: LOW — only 7 videos in recent years. Shaun included for hook pattern quality, not title pattern statistics.
